cannot_cast_message: You can't draw on the power of nature!

//: No cold armor here--trees are very vulnerable to ice breaking their limbs

descriptions.text: The forest nervously whispers to you about the presence of humans.
The forest gently whispers to you about the creatures of nature.
The forest whispers warnings of unnatural creatures nearby.

description.str: Sometimes, when an enemy punches your thorns, they hurt themselves.

//: General principle for spells below is--spells that involving growing or nurturing plants in the long-term are intelligence-based, other spells are perception-based.  Briarwall (long-term) is thus Intelligence, whereas Hungry Thirsty Roots is Perception because the roots die within minutes.
name: Commune with Nature
description: You concentrate on nature around you and commune with it, improving your health and calming the nearby wildlife.  This spell may only be cast outdoors in natural surroundings.
caster_condition_fail_message: You must be surrounded by nature to commune with nature.

name: Cultivate Goblin Fruit
description: You may spin a bit of fae magic into a plant, bringing forth a goblin fruit.  Goblin fruit have a variety of effects, never predictable but always at least somewhat beneficial.  This spell may only be cast outdoors in natural surroundings.
message: A goblin fruit swift grows from a nearby plant.
caster_condition_fail_message: You must be outdoors in natural surroundings to call forth a goblin fruit.

name: Treesinging
description: Using your voice and your magic, bring forth living wood from a tree to provide resources without harming the forest.
message: You close your eyes and sing to the trees.

name: Verdant Infusion
description: You draw on your own connection to nature and infuse some of its power into one of your allies, healing them for a small amount and increasing their overall health.

name: Roots Crack the Foundation
description: Roots can grow through even concrete given time.  With this spell, you can compress that time into a single instant to shatter walls or rip doors off their hinges.

name: Call the Children of the Roots
description: A lesser wood spirit is summoned to support you.

While in the wild, weave together some leaves and grasses and magick to form a container that will keep its contents from rotting for a length of time.  The container will only last a few weeks at most.
caster_condition_fail_message: You must be surrounded by nature and away from civiliation to cast To Withstand Decay.

name: Viridescent Vessel
description:
Trees require water but will still drown if submerged.  This spell allows the Arvore to create a floral boat with the help of a nearby tree, letting them travel safely across water.

You must be standing <color_yellow>next to a tree</color> and <color_yellow>near the water</color> for the spell to be effective.

description.str: The actual spell to summon the boat.  If you have it, it's a bug.

name: Briarwall
description: Call up thick briars and thorns, either in a line to form a wall or an arc to trap an enemy.  They will grow to full size in moments.

description.str: Call up briars and thorns, either in a line to form a wall or an arc to trap an enemy.  They will grow to full size in moments.
Call up briars and thorns, either in a line to form a wall or an arc to trap an enemy.  They will grow to full size in moments.

name: Tending the Garden
description: Invasive weeds must be rooted out, lest they choke the life from the garden.  This spell inflicts a rapid withering on plants and fungus within its area of effect.

description.str: This is the fungicidal effect of the Tending the Garden spell.  It's a bug if you have it directly.

name: Nurturing the Green
description: You can greatly speed plant growth.  Grass can grow from seed in moments, crops can be brought to ripeness, and even young trees will reach their full bloom.

name: Spreading the Viridian Mantle
description: Rather than fostering the growth of a single plant, you can cause a wave of growth all around you, affecting an entire field and bringing it towards ripeness.

name: The Forest Offers Shelter
description: In natural surroundings, you can create structures out of living wood--walls, floors, doors, and roofs all together.

description.str: The walls part of the Forest Offers Shelter spell.  It's a bug if you have it directly.
The floor part of the Forest Offers Shelter spell.  It's a bug if you have it directly.
The branches part of the Forest Offers Shelter spell.  It's a bug if you have it directly.
The doors part of the Forest Offers Shelter spell.  It's a bug if you have it directly.

name: Creeper Ascends the Wall
description: Cause a cluster of vines to burst out of diggable ground and rise up into the sky in moments.  It will be easy to climb once its growth is finished.
message: The soil churns as thick vines burst forth and shoots toward the sky.

name: Den of Brambles
description: When outside on diggable ground, create a small structure of wood near the Arvore, offering shelter against the elements.
Your command over plants allows you to cause grass to grow in profusion from the soil, making the terrain very difficult for enemies to traverse.

name.//~: The name is a reference to the poem "Goblin Market" by Christina Rossetti.

description: Your command over plants allows you to call forth roots from the earth to clutch and grasp at your enemies.

name: The Wind Blows, the Willow Bends
description: Like the flitting wind or the flexible willow, the spell allows you to avoid attacks much more easily.  It has <color_yellow>reduced effects</color> when on artificial terrain.

name: Perennial Rebirth
description: Bind your soul to the heart of the forest, rebirthing yourself if you should die.  You may only perform this in thick forests or at an arvore altar, you may be reborn no more than once in a lunar month.

name: Overgrowth
description: While outdoors and not underground, the Arvore can create a profusion of life around themselves, causing grass to grow through pavement, bushes and flowers to sprout, and trees to grow to full height in moments.

name: Fallen Trees Decay to Soil
description:
Even the mightiest tree returns to soil in the end.  The Arvore may channel this natural process into an area around themselves, infecting everyone within with the energies of rot.

<color_yellow>This spell is indiscriminate</color> and <color_red>should not be used near allies</color>.

name: Traverse the Wilds
description: Roots grow together and so forests are connected.  The Arvore may use this connection to quickly transport themselves tree to tree, stepping into an adjacent tree and then stepping out of another one within the spell's range.

name: From Root to Root the Forest Is Joined
description: All forests are connected at their hearts.  You can use this connection to travel from the depths of a forest to the depths of another forest, if you properly attune to your destination.
caster_condition_fail_message: You must be outdoors and within the depths of the forest to cast this spell.

name: Root to Root Attunement
description: Attune to a location for later traveling.  You must attune within the depths of a forest.
caster_condition_fail_message: You must be outdoors and within the depths of the forest to cast this spell.

name: Verdant Imprisonment
description: The ultimate expression of nature's power, you can turn your target into a tree that might have stood for a century or more.  It works best on an already-weakened target, and does not work at all on non-organic targets.
caster_condition_fail_message: You must be outdoors to cast Verdant Imprisonment.
target_condition_fail_message: Your target must be outdoors to transform them into a tree.

description.str: This is the banishment that the target casts on themselves.

name: Embrace of the Verdant Mother
description:
While outdoors and not underground, the Arvore can unleash the power of nature against their enemies.  The walking dead and most creatures not native to the world suffer the Earthmother's wrath, while animals and humans are healed.  Triffids, being plants, are healed as well.

Note that this power is <color_yellow>indiscriminate</color>: if the Arvore has somehow bound a Nether creature to their service, it will be damaged, and hostile humans will be healed.
